description: Open the tensile test data at ~/Documents/GeoGebra/data/tensile_test.csv containing stress (MPa) and strain (%) measurements from a 6061-T6 aluminum specimen. Enter the data as points in GeoGebra and create a scatter plot. Fit a Ramberg-Osgood model: strain(stress) = 100 * (stress/E + (stress/K)^n), using three sliders: E (50000 to 90000, step 1000), K (300 to 900, step 10), and n (3 to 30, step 1). Adjust the sliders until the model curve closely fits the experimental data through both the elastic and yielding regions. Create residual segments between each data point and the corresponding point on the model curve. Add a text element displaying the sum of squared residuals. Determine the 0.2% offset yield strength: plot the offset line g(x) = 100*x/E + 0.2, find its intersection with the model curve, and mark the yield point. Display 'Yield Stress = [value] MPa' as text. Save as tensile_analysis.ggb in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
